_comment: LLARS Demo Video - DREHBUCH / GROUND TRUTH

host.name: Alex
host.description: A friendly professional male voice, clear and articulate, with a warm tone. Natural pacing, enthusiastic but not over the top. Suitable for software demonstrations.

narrator.name: David
narrator.description: A distinguished elderly British male voice, deep and resonant, with the calm authority of a nature documentary narrator. Thoughtful pauses, gentle intonation, warm and wise. Speaking as if revealing the wonders of the natural world. Similar to Sir David Attenborough's iconic narration style.

steps.speaker: narrator
steps.narration: In the vast landscape of artificial intelligence, a new challenge has emerged. Evaluating the outputs of Large Language Models requires two distinct groups with complementary expertise.

actions.title: LLARS
actions.subtitle: A Platform for Evaluating LLM Outputs

steps.speaker: host
steps.narration: That's right. Domain experts understand what constitutes quality, while AI developers know how to structure effective prompts. Current tools force these groups to work in isolation.

steps.speaker: narrator
steps.narration: But here, we observe something remarkable. LLARS bridges this gap with three integrated modules. Let us explore this fascinating ecosystem.

steps.speaker: host
steps.narration: Let's start with Collaborative Prompt Engineering. Here, domain experts and AI developers work together in real-time.

steps.speaker: host
steps.narration: I'll create a new prompt for news article summarization. Watch how changes are tracked at the character level.

steps.speaker: host
steps.narration: I'll add a system block to define the LLM's role. As a journalist, I specify that summaries must be exactly two sentences, factually accurate, and written in neutral tone.

actions.text:
You are an expert news editor creating concise summaries.
Your summaries must be:
- Exactly 2 sentences long
- Factually accurate
- Written in neutral tone

steps.speaker: narrator
steps.narration: And here we witness the remarkable flexibility of the system. Variables in double curly braces become placeholders, ready to be filled with data during batch generation.

steps.speaker: host
steps.narration: Each user can test the prompt independently. The test dialog lets us select a model and generate a response.

steps.speaker: narrator
steps.narration: With the prompt refined, we now venture into the realm of Batch Generation. This module automates output creation at an impressive scale.

steps.speaker: host
steps.narration: The wizard guides you through source selection. Upload data, link existing scenarios, or use prompt-only mode for quick tests.

steps.speaker: narrator
steps.narration: The system calculates all possible combinations and estimates costs before execution. A prudent approach to resource management.

steps.speaker: host
steps.narration: Now let's explore the Evaluation Hub. This is where human evaluators and LLM judges assess the generated outputs.

steps.speaker: host
steps.narration: The Scenario Manager lets you create evaluation scenarios with different types. Ranking, rating, comparison, and more.

steps.speaker: narrator
steps.narration: LLARS supports multiple evaluation methodologies. Ranking for relative comparison. Rating for absolute scores. And pairwise comparison for rigorous A B testing.

steps.speaker: narrator
steps.narration: And so we conclude our journey through LLARS. Open-source under MIT license. Requiring Docker and 16 gigabytes of RAM. Visit the repository to begin your own exploration. Thank you for watching.
